name: White Bread

pros.title: works as a practical staple
pros.explanation: Grains earn real credit for affordability and usability.

pros.title: easy to build meals around
pros.explanation: A grain that works in real life is more valuable than one that only looks good on paper.

pros.title: easy to cook at scale
pros.explanation: That practicality matters for a staple category.

cons.title: refined bread format gives up too much of the grain upside
cons.explanation: It still has a role, but it is not a strong category performer.

cons.title: carb load stays high
cons.explanation: That is the basic tradeoff almost every grain has to manage.

cons.title: rarely finishes the meal alone
cons.explanation: Most grains still need pairing to feel nutritionally complete.

sourceNotes: Values are approximate representative placeholders for ruleset tuning, not a clinical nutrient database.
